hero_tagline: Planning your family getaway to Apollo Bay means finding that perfect holiday park where the kids can explore while you unwind, creating those precious memories along the stunning Great Ocean Road.
heading: Best Family Holiday & Caravan Parks in Apollo Bay
hero_intro:
Planning a family trip to Apollo Bay? We compared the best family holiday and caravan parks in Apollo Bay so you can quickly find the right park for your kids, budget and travel style.
Apollo Bay is home to the highest-scoring holiday park on the entire Great Ocean Road, and its position roughly midway along the drive makes it the natural base for a family road trip.
local_knowledge: BIG4 Apollo Bay Pisces is the standout, scoring 93/100 with a jumping pillow, water play, pump track and heated pool right by the beach, genuinely resort-level facilities. Apollo Bay Holiday Park is the town's other solid option, with a retro arcade and games room. Marengo Family Caravan Park suits families chasing wildlife, seals and koalas are regularly spotted, plus good rock pool exploring. Kennett River, 20 minutes north, is the most reliable wild koala spotting on the whole Great Ocean Road. The Great Ocean Walk starts here for families wanting a longer coastal hike, though shorter sections suit younger kids well too.

if_we_were_booking.label: The full resort experience
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose BIG4 Apollo Bay Pisces Holiday Park without hesitation, the highest-scored park on the Great Ocean Road, with a jumping pillow, water play, pump track and heated pool by the beach.

if_we_were_booking.label: Wildlife and rock pools
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose Marengo Family Caravan Park for direct beach access, rock pool exploring and regular seal and koala sightings.

if_we_were_booking.label: Our tip
if_we_were_booking.text: Base at Pisces for two or three nights and use it as your Great Ocean Road headquarters, the Twelve Apostles are about 90 minutes west and Kennett River's koalas are 20 minutes north, both easy day trips.

faq.question: Is Apollo Bay good for families?
faq.answer: Yes, it's arguably the best family base on the entire Great Ocean Road, with the highest-scoring park in the region and a central position for day trips.

faq.question: Which Apollo Bay park is best for kids?
faq.answer: BIG4 Apollo Bay Pisces Holiday Park, scoring 93/100, with a jumping pillow, water play area, pump track and heated pool.

faq.question: Where can kids see wild koalas near Apollo Bay?
faq.answer: Kennett River, about 20 minutes north, is the most reliable wild koala spotting spot on the Great Ocean Road.

faq.question: Is Marengo good for young children?
faq.answer: Yes, it offers direct beach access, rock pool exploring at low tide and a good chance of spotting seals and koalas nearby.

faq.question: How far are the Twelve Apostles from Apollo Bay?
faq.answer: About 90 minutes drive west, an easy day trip with the best light in late afternoon.

faq.question: Is the beach safe for swimming?
faq.answer: Apollo Bay's main beach is patrolled in season and suits family swimming.

faq.question: What are the best free family activities?
faq.answer: Rock pooling at Marengo, koala spotting at Kennett River, and shorter sections of the Great Ocean Walk.

faq.question: When is the best time to visit?
faq.answer: Summer for swimming, though the Great Ocean Road is scenic year-round and school holidays bring the biggest crowds.

faq.question: What should families do when it rains?
faq.answer: BIG4 Apollo Bay Pisces' indoor facilities and Apollo Bay Holiday Park's games room and arcade cover a wet day.

faq.question: How far ahead should you book?
faq.answer: BIG4 Apollo Bay Pisces books out well ahead for school holidays given its reputation as the top park on the coast.
